mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has held near a cycle-high plateau (5.25%+) for over eight months. Persistently restrictive policy rates raise the discount rate on future equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, producing a systematic drag on S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as valuation multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26 percent, unchanged for several months as of t-1mo
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS above 5% are not significantly more negative than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts have declined materially from cycle peaks by early 2007. Once HOUST falls more than 15% below its trailing 12-month high, this signals an accelerating residential investment contraction that historically precedes broader demand deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as markets price weaker consumer and construction-linked earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 15% below its trailing 12-month peak level
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1525 thousand units, down sharply from prior cycle peaks near 2000+
failure_condition: In instances where the HOUST trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration where PAYEMS month-over-month growth slows below its 6-month trailing average while UNRATE simultaneously ticks up by at least 0.1pp signals an inflecting labor market that is not captured by either series alone. This joint signal anticipates weakening consumer income growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth below its 6-month trailing average AND UNRATE rises >= 0.1pp in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4 percent, near cycle lows as of t-1mo
failure_condition: When both conditions co-occur, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects healthy factory-sector output and supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next trading week as the signal is incorporated into forward earnings estimates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.1433, indicating continued expansion as of t-2mo
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO prints are not significantly higher than following negative prints (p > 0.05), or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where the Fed has held rates restrictive for an extended period, continued strength in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending that masks underlying credit-driven fragility. In this regime the positive demand signal is discounted by markets anticipating a policy-induced slowdown, producing muted or negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter despite the headline strength.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367539 million dollars showing continued growth despite FEDFUNDS at 5.26 percent
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion under restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of strong RSAFS growth combined with FEDFUNDS above 5% are not significantly negative versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign outside late-cycle regimes without regime interaction explaining it.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing year-over-year CPI inflation by more than 2 percentage points, real policy rates are historically elevated and restrictive, prompting near-term equity market weakness over the following week as investors reprice tightening financial conditions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rate exceeds 2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26 percent versus CPIAUCSL level 203.906 implying moderate real rate gap
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on the real-rate trigger are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or trigger fires fewer than 10 times in the historical sample making the test underpowered.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining housing starts and a high federal funds rate jointly signals a credit-constrained housing contraction that is more severe than either series indicates alone, because elevated financing costs amplify the demand shock from falling housing activity. This joint deterioration transmits through mortgage-linked credit channels and financial sector earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declining year-over-year AND FEDFUNDS above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1525 thousand units declining while FEDFUNDS remains at 5.26 percent
regime_hint: housing downturn combined with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: When both conditions are active jointly, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative than when only one condition holds (p > 0.05), indicating no genuine interaction effect beyond additive single-series contributions.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is at or near a multi-decade cyclical low (below 4.5%), the labor market has limited room for further improvement and historically exhibits mean-reverting dynamics. In this regime, low UNRATE readings are a lagging indicator of a maturing expansion and are associated with weaker subsequent S&P 500 returns over the following month as forward-looking markets anticipate labor market cooling.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4 percent as of t-1mo, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during sub-4.5% UNRATE regimes are not significantly lower than returns during higher-UNRATE regimes (p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window within this regime.
